name: Cerebellum
location: Posterior cranial fossa inferior to occipital lobes and dorsal to brainstem
description: Contains over 50% of all brain neurons packed into high-density Purkinje cell circuits, coordinating smooth motor timing and balance.
functions: Motor coordination, error correction, and movement smoothing
Motor skill learning via climbing fiber long-term depression (LTD)
Cognitive timing and linguistic rhythm prediction
bloodSupply: SCA, AICA, and PICA (Superior, Anterior Inferior, and Posterior Inferior Cerebellar Arteries)
clinicalImportance: Lesions cause ipsilateral limb ataxia, dysdiadochokinesia, and scanning staccato speech.
researchNotes: Contains ~80 billion cerebellar granule cells, making it the highest neuronal density structure in the human body.
interestingFacts: Account for only 10% of total brain volume, but holds over 50% of the entire brain's neurons.
